agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"E-commerce platform managing the full purchase lifecycle: browsing, carting, checkout with inventory reservation, payment, and post-purchase refunds. The system must balance inventory accuracy, promotional pricing integrity, and order immutability after payment.","bounded_context":"Order & Checkout","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Cart","definition":"A temporary, uncommitted collection of items a customer intends to purchase. No inventory is reserved at this stage."},{"term":"Checkout","definition":"The process initiated when a customer commits to purchasing cart contents. Triggers inventory reservation and starts the payment window."},{"term":"Inventory Reservation","definition":"A time-bound hold on inventory units, created at checkout start and released if payment is not completed within 15 minutes."},{"term":"Abandoned Checkout","definition":"A checkout session where no payment confirmation is received within the 15-minute reservation window."},{"term":"Promotion","definition":"A seller-configured pricing discount applied to a specific product. Only one may be active per product at any time."},{"term":"Discount Code","definition":"A buyer-supplied code that reduces order cost. Mutually exclusive with promotional pricing on the same order."},{"term":"Payment Confirmation","definition":"The irreversible event after which an order becomes immutable."},{"term":"Refund Window","definition":"The 30-day period following delivery during which a customer may request a refund."},{"term":"Listed Product","definition":"A product visible in the catalog. Visibility does not imply purchasability — zero-inventory listed products cannot be bought."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Inventory is not reserved when items are added to the cart; reservation occurs only when checkout begins.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Premature reservation would corrupt inventory counts for other customers, leading to false stock-outs. The cart-vs-checkout boundary is a fundamental system integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"Adding an item to a cart MUST NOT decrement available inventory or create any reservation record."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"If payment is not confirmed within 15 minutes of checkout start, the inventory reservation is released.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 15-minute window is a business policy threshold. It could be adjusted (e.g., to 10 or 20 minutes) without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"Any reservation older than 15 minutes without a corresponding payment confirmation MUST be released, restoring the reserved units to available inventory."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"A product may have at most one active promotion at any given time.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Multiple simultaneous promotions on the same product would create ambiguous pricing — the system cannot deterministically resolve which price to display. This is a data consistency invariant.","testable_assertion":"Attempting to activate a second promotion on a product that already has an active promotion MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Discount codes and promotional pricing are mutually exclusive per order — only one discount mechanism applies.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Stacking discounts would violate pricing integrity and could produce below-cost pricing. The system must enforce a single discount path to maintain financial correctness.","testable_assertion":"An order containing a product with active promotional pricing MUST reject application of a discount code, and vice versa."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Orders cannot be modified after payment is confirmed.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Post-payment immutability is a financial integrity constraint. Modifications after payment would desynchronize the order record from the actual financial transaction.","testable_assertion":"Any attempt to add, remove, or change items/quantities on an order with a confirmed payment MUST be rejected."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"A product with zero available inventory cannot be purchased, even if it remains listed in the catalog.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Selling non-existent inventory would create fulfillment obligations that cannot be met — a fundamental physical constraint.","testable_assertion":"Checkout initiation MUST fail for any line item whose product has zero available inventory at the moment reservation is attempted."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"Refund requests must be submitted within 30 days of delivery.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 30-day window is a customer service policy. Management could extend or shorten it without corrupting system data.","testable_assertion":"A refund request submitted more than 30 days after the recorded delivery date MUST be rejected."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"A customer has items in their cart","when":"The customer has not yet initiated checkout","then":"Available inventory for those products remains unchanged and no reservation records exist"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"A customer initiates checkout with items in their cart","when":"All items have sufficient available inventory","then":"Inventory is reserved for each line item, the reservation timestamp is recorded, and available inventory is decremented accordingly"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"A customer initiates checkout","when":"Any item in the cart has zero available inventory","then":"Checkout is rejected with a clear indication of which product(s) are unavailable"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"A checkout session has been started with inventory reserved","when":"15 minutes elapse without payment confirmation","then":"All reservations for that session are released and available inventory is restored"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"A checkout session is active and within the 15-minute window","when":"Payment is confirmed","then":"The order is created, reservations are converted to committed allocations, and the order becomes immutable"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An order has confirmed payment","when":"Any modification is attempted (add, remove, change quantity)","then":"The modification is rejected and the order remains unchanged"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"A product already has an active promotion","when":"An administrator attempts to activate a second promotion on the same product","then":"The activation is rejected and the existing promotion remains unchanged"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A customer's cart contains a product with an active promotion","when":"The customer attempts to apply a discount code to the order","then":"The discount code is rejected with a message explaining that promotional pricing and discount codes cannot be combined"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A customer's order has a discount code applied","when":"A product with active promotional pricing is added to the order","then":"Either the discount code is removed or the promotional item is added at its promotional price with the discount code not applying to it — the system enforces mutual exclusivity"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An order was delivered 29 days ago","when":"The customer submits a refund request","then":"The refund request is accepted for processing"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"An order was delivered 31 days ago","when":"The customer submits a refund request","then":"The refund request is rejected as outside the eligible window"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"A product has zero inventory","when":"The product is displayed in the catalog","then":"The product is visible but clearly marked as unavailable for purchase, and the add-to-cart or buy action is blocked or the checkout will fail"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Two customers attempt to reserve the last unit of a product simultaneously at checkout start","expected_behavior":"Only one reservation succeeds; the second customer receives an out-of-stock error. Concurrency control must be enforced at the inventory reservation level."},{"scenario":"Payment confirmation arrives at exactly the 15-minute boundary","expected_behavior":"The system must define whether the boundary is inclusive or exclusive. A consistent rule (e.g., strictly less than 15 minutes) must be enforced. Race conditions between the reservation-release timer and payment confirmation must be handled atomically."},{"scenario":"A product's inventory drops to zero after items are added to cart but before checkout","expected_behavior":"Checkout initiation fails for that item since reservation happens at checkout, not at cart addition."},{"scenario":"A promotion is deactivated after a customer has already started checkout with promotional pricing","expected_behavior":"The price locked at checkout start should be honored for that session. The promotion deactivation applies to new sessions only."},{"scenario":"Inventory is restored from an abandoned checkout while another customer is mid-checkout for the same product","expected_behavior":"The restored inventory becomes immediately available. The second customer's checkout should see updated availability."},{"scenario":"A refund is requested exactly on day 30 after delivery","expected_behavior":"The system must define whether day 30 is inclusive (refund allowed) or exclusive. The boundary condition must be explicit."},{"scenario":"Customer applies a discount code, then a promotion is activated on a product already in their cart before they check out","expected_behavior":"At checkout, the mutual exclusivity rule must be enforced with the most current state — the system must detect the conflict and resolve it."},{"scenario":"Partial inventory availability — cart has 5 units but only 3 are available at checkout","expected_behavior":"The spec does not explicitly address partial fulfillment. The system should either reject checkout entirely or offer to adjust quantity — this needs clarification."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Correct inventory reservation prevents overselling (costly fulfillment failures and refunds). Mutual exclusivity of discounts protects margins. The 15-minute timeout balances conversion rate against inventory lock-up cost.","operational":"Automated reservation release reduces manual intervention. Immutable post-payment orders simplify fulfillment pipeline and audit trails. Single-promotion-per-product simplifies pricing engine logic.","user":"Clear feedback on inventory availability and discount conflicts prevents frustrating checkout failures. The 30-day refund window provides post-purchase confidence.","risk":"Race conditions at the reservation boundary (15-min expiry vs. payment) are the highest technical risk. Incorrect discount stacking is the highest financial risk. Catalog display of zero-inventory items may cause customer frustration if not clearly communicated.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Customers","impact":"Direct experience impact: cart behavior, checkout failures on out-of-stock, discount clarity, refund eligibility window"},{"group":"Merchandising/Marketing Team","impact":"Promotion management constrained to one-per-product; discount code strategy limited by mutual exclusivity rule"},{"group":"Finance/Accounting","impact":"Order immutability post-payment simplifies reconciliation; refund window bounds liability exposure"},{"group":"Warehouse/Fulfillment","impact":"Inventory accuracy depends on correct reservation lifecycle; overselling creates fulfillment exceptions"},{"group":"Customer Support","impact":"Must enforce 30-day refund boundary; needs clear escalation path for edge cases (e.g., day-30 boundary, abandoned checkout complaints)"}]},"confidence":{"level":78,"reasoning":"The spec is well-structured and internally consistent with no contradictions. Most rules are clearly stated with explicit boundaries. Confidence is not higher because several boundary conditions and resolution strategies are left implicit (partial fulfillment, exact boundary inclusivity, price-lock behavior at checkout).","high_confidence_areas":["Inventory reservation lifecycle (cart vs checkout)","Post-payment order immutability","Single promotion per product constraint","Discount mutual exclusivity principle"],"low_confidence_areas":["Whether the 15-minute and 30-day boundaries are inclusive or exclusive","How partial inventory availability at checkout is handled (all-or-nothing vs. partial fulfillment)","Price-locking semantics: is the price locked at checkout start or at payment confirmation?","Resolution priority when a discount code is already applied and a promotional product is added mid-session (AC-9 offers two possible behaviors)","Whether the refund window is measured from delivery confirmation timestamp or calendar date"],"assumptions":["Inventory is tracked as discrete integer units","The 15-minute timer starts from the moment checkout is initiated, not from some other event","Delivery date is a recorded system event with a precise timestamp","Discount code and promotional pricing conflict is evaluated at the order level, not per line item"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
